name: Quiroga – tour around high Boi and the rivers Lor and Sil
name_original: Quiroga – vuelta por alto de Boi y los ríos Lor y Sil
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Quiroga traverse the diverse landscapes of Lugo, Galicia, characterized by deep river valleys, terraced vineyards, and mountainous terrain. The region is situated within the Ribeira Sacra, featuring the Lor and Sil river canyons and the proximity to the Serra do Courel mountain range. Cyclists encounter varied elevations, from riverine paths to viewpoints like Miradoiro de Bustelo, and pass through ancient chestnut forests. Best touring cycling routes around Quiroga The most popular touring cycling route is Quiroga – tour around high Boi and the rivers Lor and Sil, a difficult 37.0 miles (59.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 38 minutes to complete, offering views of the Lor and Sil river valleys. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Castro Caldelas – tour around Pobra de Trives – Special Conservation Zone river Navea, a difficult 43.5 miles (70.0 km) path. This route explores the area around Pobra de Trives and the Navea river's Special Conservation Zone. Local touring cyclists also love the A Pobra de Trives Town Center – Chaira das Lamas Viewpoint loop from O Barrio dos Xitanos, a 30.8 miles (49.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain to the Chaira das Lamas Viewpoint, often completed in about 3 hours 42 minutes. Touring cycling around Quiroga is defined by dramatic river canyons, terraced vineyards, and mountainous ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes present significant elevation changes. The routes in Quiroga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 60 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Quiroga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Quiroga?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes around Quiroga, offering diverse experiences through the region's landscapes. These routes cater to various skill levels, though many feature significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Quiroga?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Quiroga is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is milder and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be warm, especially in the river valleys, while winters might bring cooler temperatures and potential for rain, particularly in the mountainous areas of Serra do Courel.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Quiroga?
A: The touring cycling routes in Quiroga are predominantly challenging, with 11 out of 13 routes rated as 'difficult' and 2 as 'moderate'. These routes often involve significant elevation gains and descents, traversing dramatic river canyons and mountainous terrain. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018822231/quiroga-tour-around-high-boi-and-the-rivers-lor-and-sil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quiroga – tour around high Boi and the rivers Lor and Sil&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 60 km with over 1600 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Quiroga?
A: The touring cycling routes in Quiroga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning natural beauty of the Ribeira Sacra, the challenging yet rewarding terrain, and the opportunity to explore areas like the Lor and Sil river valleys.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Quiroga?
A: Yes, several touring cycling routes in Quiroga are designed as loops. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Pobra de Trives Town Center – Chaira das Lamas Viewpoint loop from O Barrio dos Xitanos&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 49.5 km difficult route that takes you through varied terrain to the scenic Chaira das Lamas Viewpoint.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Quiroga?
A: While many routes in Quiroga are rated as difficult due to significant elevation changes, the region's section of the 'Camino de Invierno' offers stages with lower difficulty on asphalted roads with minimal traffic, which can be more suitable for families or those seeking a less strenuous ride. It's advisable to check individual route profiles for specific elevation and distance details.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes in Quiroga?
A: Quiroga's touring cycling routes offer access to a wealth of natural landmarks. You can encounter the dramatic canyons of the Lor and Sil rivers, explore ancient chestnut forests, and visit viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pena de Matacás viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. You might also discover waterfalls such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deer Spring Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beiro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions near the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Quiroga is rich in history. Cyclists can explore the historic-artistic site of Castro Caldelas, with its charming old town and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5014945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Caldelas Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The Camino de Invierno, which passes through Quiroga, also follows a secondary Roman road that once went through the ancient Tunnel de Montefurado, a testament to Roman engineering.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Quiroga?
A: Quiroga, being a cycle-friendly region, generally offers parking options in and around the main towns and trailheads. For specific routes, it's recommended to check the starting points for available parking facilities. The low traffic volumes in Galicia also contribute to a more relaxed experience for cyclists and drivers alike.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes in Quiroga using public transport?
A: While specific public transport options directly to every trailhead might be limited, Quiroga is a municipality within Lugo, Galicia, which has regional bus services. The 'Camino de Invierno' route, which passes through Quiroga, is well-integrated into the region's infrastructure, and some sections may be accessible via public transport, though carrying bikes might require prior arrangement or specific services.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Ribeira Sacra region specifically?
A: Yes, Quiroga is situated within the breathtaking Ribeira Sacra region. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017320522/castro-caldelas-tour-around-pobra-de-trives-special-conservation-zone-river-navea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Caldelas – tour around Pobra de Trives – Special Conservation Zone river Navea&lt;/a&gt; offer a deep dive into this area, showcasing its dramatic river canyons, terraced vineyards, and unique geological formations.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Quiroga?
A: The terrain around Quiroga is highly varied, ranging from deep river valleys along the Lor and Sil rivers to the mountainous landscapes of the Serra do Courel. You can expect a mix of asphalted roads, especially on sections of the Camino de Invierno, and potentially some older stony paths, particularly if venturing towards routes associated with the Ribeira Sacra MTB Centre. Significant ascents and descents are common.
tips: On the way up from Quiroga, the mountain range of Courel welcomes you, surrounded by a protected forest landscape and incredible views. The rocky formation and the cliff front are of notable geological importance.
Small town located in the Sierra del Caurel in Lugo. You arrive at the village after a long climb up a undulating road that goes up the mountain.
Froxán is a small mountain town in the Courel mountain range and is one of the access points to the Lor River.
